question: The chain of command in an organisation
options: Few levels of hierarchy and a wide span of control
To ensure consistent messaging across the organisation
The hierarchy through which authority and instructions pass
Contracting external providers to perform certain business functions
answer: The hierarchy through which authority and instructions pass

question: Delegation of authority means
options: To compare organisational performance against best practice standards
Maintaining communication, security and productivity
Assigning tasks and responsibility to a subordinate
To ensure consistent messaging across the organisation
answer: Assigning tasks and responsibility to a subordinate

question: The span of control refers to
options: The number of subordinates a manager directly supervises
Assigning tasks and responsibility to a subordinate
A group of employees who meet regularly to identify and solve work problems
Contracting external providers to perform certain business functions
answer: The number of subordinates a manager directly supervises

question: A flat organisational structure has
options: Functional and project-based management
Few levels of hierarchy and a wide span of control
The hierarchy through which authority and instructions pass
A group of employees who meet regularly to identify and solve work problems
answer: Few levels of hierarchy and a wide span of control

question: A tall organisational structure has
options: Assigning tasks and responsibility to a subordinate
Many levels of hierarchy and a narrow span of control
A group of employees who meet regularly to identify and solve work problems
Contracting external providers to perform certain business functions
answer: Many levels of hierarchy and a narrow span of control

question: The difference between authority and responsibility
options: The number of subordinates a manager directly supervises
Authority is the right to act; responsibility is the obligation to perform
A group of employees who meet regularly to identify and solve work problems
Contracting external providers to perform certain business functions
answer: Authority is the right to act; responsibility is the obligation to perform

question: Matrix organisational structure combines
options: Functional and project-based management
Assigning tasks and responsibility to a subordinate
The number of subordinates a manager directly supervises

options: The number of subordinates a manager directly supervises

Authority is the right to act; responsibility is the obligation to perform

options: Few levels of hierarchy and a wide span of control
